info.parenttitle: A3.2 Introduction to structural systems (HL only)

markdown:
# Structural Components


1. A **structural component** is a part of a structure that **supports loads** and **resists forces**.
2. The most common components are **beams** (horizontal) and **columns** (vertical), each helping to **keep the structure stable and upright**.

## Beams


1. **Beams** are **horizontal structural elements** that carry loads across a span.
2. They resist **bending** forces and transfer loads to supports.

<callout type="note">There are different types of beams based on **how they are supported**:

* Simply supported beam
* Fixed beam
* Cantilever beam
* Continuously supported beam</callout>

### Simply Supported Beams


<definition term="Simply Supported Beam">A beam that is supported at either end.</definition>

1. **Load Distribution**: Transfers loads to the supports.
2. **Flexibility**: Allows for slight movement at the supports, reducing stress.

<callout type="example">Bridges, floors, and roofs

![Simple supported beam used in a roof (Ernst, 2024)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d4bd96ec0b0cbc216fb8eeff66fe9e8954e22261-1665x1150.png)</callout>

<callout type="note">**Simply supported beams** are the most common type of beam used in construction due to their simplicity and efficiency.</callout>

### Fixed Beams


<definition term="Fixed Beam">A member in a structure that is fixed on both ends to supports.</definition>

1. **Stability**: Provides greater resistance to bending and deflection.
2. **Load Capacity**: Can support heavier loads compared to simply supported beams.

<callout type="example">High-rise buildings and industrial structures

![Fixed beam used in construction of large buildings](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/54adb48dac08d9c48ac8d758ba23d6fa36642f69-490x300.png)</callout>

### Cantilever Beams


<definition term="Cantilever Beam">A rigid structure that is **fixed on one end** and **extends horizontally without support on the other**.</definition>

1. **Overhanging Structures**: Ideal for balconies, bridges, and canopies.
2. **Load Bearing**: Supports loads without additional supports at the free end.

<callout type="example">Balconies, overpasses, and signage

![One Za-abeel in Dubai is the longest cantilever in the world (dezeen)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/16524aaf9ca8fa8f371e23d66f1d0b671c809d65-2364x1329.png)</callout>

<callout type="analogy">Consider a diving board: the **fixed** end is **anchored**, while the **free** end **extends** **outward**, supporting the diver's weight.</callout>

### Continuously Supported Beams


<definition term="Continuously Supported Beam">A beam that is supported at **more than two points** along its length.</definition>

1. **Load Distribution**: Spreads loads evenly across multiple supports.
2. **Reduced Deflection**: Minimizes bending and sagging.

<callout type="example">Long-span bridges and multi-story buildings

![Bridge that uses a continuously supported beam structure (howscienceworks)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/1edfdaa84a82da66177106585cbb42eee16cea68-828x466.png)</callout>

<callout type="note">**Continuously supported beams** are more efficient in distributing loads than simply supported beams, making them ideal for longer spans.</callout>

## Columns


<definition term="Column">An **upright pillar** typically used to support beams in structures.</definition>

1. **Load Transfer**: Carries compressive loads to the ground.
2. **Stability**: Provides structural support and prevents collapse.

<callout type="example">Skyscrapers, bridges, and residential buildings

![Columns used in the Temple of Hercules Victor, the oldest building in Rome (lions in the piazza)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/b283b95761c6ba53920cff2210d51ad934531329-1024x768.png)</callout>

<callout type="warning">* Don't confuse **columns** with **beams**. 
* Columns are vertical and primarily handle compressive loads, while beams are horizontal and support bending loads.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Saying a structure “holds up weight” without explaining **how** it transfers or resists force</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Identify the **beams and/or columns** in the structure.
* State the **type of beam.**
* Explain how it **resists forces** or supports the design

![Scaffolding](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/7d5e4d372d5421caf65a0ce8fd2c86d40477a88b-1100x1100.png)</callout>

****
